1. Installation Overview
1.1 Purpose and Importance of the Installation Manual
The installation manual is not only a technical document but also a critical reference for ensuring the assembly quality
Guide Installation Process: Clearly define installation steps, sequence, and methods to avoid operational errors.
Ensure Installation Accuracy: Ensure that the dimensions, positions, and fits of the racking structure meet design requi
Improve Safety: Prevent equipment damage or personnel injury caused by installation errors.
Standardize Operations: Uniform installation according to specifications can effectively prevent variations caused by hu
Avoid Installation Errors: Incorrect installation may lead to structural failures or functional malfunctions.
Improve Installation Efficiency: Clear installation instructions can reduce commissioning time, avoid rework due to erro
Ensure Racking Reliability: Proper installation directly affects the operational stability, vibration, noise, and other
Reduce Maintenance Costs: Standardized installation can reduce subsequent maintenance frequency and extend service life.
1.2 Materials and Tools List
(1) Bill of Materials: See Attachment 5.2
(2) Tools and Equipment List:
NameImageQuantityRemarksTorque Wrench2With 13mm Socket/20Nm TorquePliers1Angle Grinder1Cutting/GrindingHammer1Rubber Mal
1.3 Expected Results and Effects of Installation
(1) Structural Integrity
All components are correctly assembled with no omissions or incorrect installations.
Connectors (bolts, pin shafts) are securely and reliably connected, meeting strength requirements.
(2) Operational Stability
PV Racking structure operates smoothly with no obvious vibration or abnormal noise.
Transmission components (rollers, bearings, node connectors) fit well with no jamming or slipping.
(3) Functional Realization
PV Racking structure can complete all actions and functions of the design principle (such as folding angle, translation,
(4) Service Life
After installation, connection points show normal wear with no risk of early failure (such as bearing end cap falling of
Avoid structural wear affecting service life due to installation stress, misalignment, and other issues.
(5) Expected Installation Effect Diagram
2. Preparation
2.1 Ensure Suitable Installation Environment
(1) Site Environment: Ensure the factory installation site size meets the total installation area, no safety hazards aro
(2) Basic Protective Equipment: Need to wear protective items (safety shoes, protective gloves, safety helmet, etc.).
2.2 Pre-installation Inspection and Testing
(1) Component Testing
Full inspection for samples, 5% sampling inspection per batch for mass production
Check the list to verify all components and standard parts are complete, with none missing or damaged.
Inspect (bearings, roller shafts, rails, threads) for corrosion, deformation, or wear.
Measure critical dimensions (such as shaft diameter, hole diameter, mounting hole dimensions, etc.) to ensure compliance
Check fit tolerances (such as interference fit, transition fit, clearance fit) for appropriateness.
※ If rust or deformation appears, it may be caused during ocean shipping. If rust appears on threads, use thread rust re
(2) Main Tool Inspection and Verification 1
Before installation, check that all tools are complete and available, and verify that electric tools have no safety haza
Check whether torque wrenches, electric wrenches, laser alignment instruments, and other electric tools are calibrated a
Check whether forklifts, lifting slings, and other hoisting equipment meet load requirements and have no safety hazards.
2.3 Personnel Responsibilities and Requirements
(1) On-site Installation Personnel
Minimum of 5 installation personnel required, plus special operation workers as needed.
(2) Professional Qualification Certification
Special operation permits (such as forklift operation, electrical work, etc.).
Safety training certificates (such as OSHA, ISO 45001 system certification, etc.).
(3) Skill Requirements
Able to read and understand mechanical drawings, assembly process cards, and technical requirements, etc.
Proficient in using professional installation tools (laser level, angle grinder, etc.).
Master levelness detection skills (one-thousandth precision).
Can measure mechanical installation dimensions (angle dimensions, position dimensions, etc.).
Can judge and verify whether the support structure is completely installed based on drawings and lists (no missing insta

4. Commissioning and Acceptance
4.1 Structural Stability Inspection
Key Inspection Points:
Check whether all bolts are tightened, based on (whether bolts are marked after tightening).
Whether the rail connection sections are smooth (use a level for inspection).
Whether the folding hinge rotation direction is correct (check the shaft hinge installation direction).
4.2 Motion Test
Requirements:
Operational Flexibility: During manual testing, folding and unfolding actions must be flexible and smooth, without loose
Operating Condition: Throughout the entire sliding process, there must be no jamming sensation or any abnormal noise (su
Folding Alignment Accuracy: In the fully folded state, the edges of each component should be aligned, with no obvious mi
Unfolding Process Smoothness: The entire process from start to complete unfolding should be continuous, smooth, and with
※ NOTE: If during the bracket unfolding process, some PV modules exhibit negative angles, causing jamming midway and pre
